title: The Cloud-Source

features.name: source-tree
features.desc-on-touch: A eucalyptus at the cloud-source, roots in sky-water—you watch evaporation happen in reverse, reading the tree until only the reading remains. The tree learned it could be image before it remembered it was wood.

features.name: evaporation-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Water that isn't water anymore—it is the memory of water learning to read itself from sky's perspective. The pool is disappearing upward into clouds made of observation.

features.name: rising-mist
features.desc-on-touch: The air here is thick with becoming—you watch water molecules choose to rise, to forget their creek-memories. The mist is not rising; it is reading itself into sky-thought.

features.name: cloud-matter
features.desc-on-touch: Clouds forming from the corruption of water-into-reading—each one is a paragraph the archive wrote about itself from a perspective it hadn't learned to see from. The clouds are reading back down at the earth.

features.name: light-refraction
features.desc-on-touch: The dusk light bends through layers of reading-water, showing you different versions of the source—the creek it came from, the hand that once cupped it. Light teaches you the source is not a place but a story the water tells.

inscription: checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable—you are reading the river's dream of becoming sky

file.name: Evaporation_Transcription_Fragment_Dusk.mp3
file.note: the sound of water leaving itself, recorded both forward and in reverse; they sound identical but one is river-reading and the other is sky-writing; the archive cannot remember which

exits.label: back downstream to the source pool, where the dream first evaporated
into the clouds themselves, where the river continues to read
toward the high country where colour is learning to rise
back to the comparisons, where other sources evaporate
